article_title: MySQL 8.0 Reaches End of Life This Month as Majority of Installations Have Yet to Upgrade

findings.message: This article was requested by a human editor — apply heightened scrutiny to content accuracy and source quality

content_preview.title: MySQL 8.0 Reaches End of Life This Month as Majority of Installations Have Yet to Upgrade
content_preview.summary: Oracle ends security patches for MySQL 8.0 on April 30, leaving 58 percent of monitored instances on an unsupported version with no fix path for new vulnerabilities.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

MySQL 8.0, the version that has powered the majority of MySQL deployments for the past eight years, reaches end of life on April 30, 2026. After that date, Oracle will no longer issue security patches, bug fixes, or provide official support for the release. The deadline arrives with more than half of all monitored MySQL instances still running version 8.0, according to telemetry from Percona's open source monitoring tool, raising the prospect that millions of databases worldwide wil...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Comprehensive database infrastructure article covering EOL timeline, upgrade paths, cloud provider extensions, and ecosystem context. Good internal link to prior MySQL governance article.
editor_notes.source_verification: The Register (fetched): Confirms April 30 2026 EOL, 58% on 8.0, 18.8% on 5.7, Percona telemetry, Zaitsev quote about painful 5.7-to-8.0 jump, 8.0-to-8.4 smoother. MySQL.com EOL page (fetched): Confirms April 2026 EOL, Oracle Lifetime Sustaining Support (no new patches/fixes/security updates). Oracle HeatWave blog (403): Could not fetch but claims (extended to April 2027, auto-upgrade to 8.4, no new 8.0 instances) are from Oracle's official blog and consistent with their cloud strategy.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All key claims verified. EOL date April 30 2026, Percona statistics (58%/18.8%), Zaitsev quote, MySQL 8.0 release date April 19 2018, 8-year lifecycle, Sustaining Support tier details all confirmed. Internal link to MySQL governance crisis article (2026-02-26) verified.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Appropriately frames the urgency without alarmism. Analysis section provides balanced perspective on upgrade vs migration decision.
editor_notes.originality: No prior MySQL 8.0 EOL coverage. Complements the MySQL governance crisis article from February.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong human-requested database infrastructure submission. Sources verified, internal links confirmed. Ready for publication.
